About
Direct Pumping

A North Texas septic and environmental pumping company built on doing the job right, being straight with customers, and showing up when it counts.

Our Services
Our Story

Built in
Johnson County.

Direct Pumping was founded right here in Johnson County, Texas — and that matters. We're not a franchise, not a call center dispatching strangers, and not a company that treats every customer like a ticket number.

We started Direct Pumping because we saw how the industry was being done — and knew it could be done better. Better communication. More honest pricing. Actually showing up on time. Treating a homeowner's property with the same care you'd want someone to take with yours.

Over the past several years we've grown our operation, expanded our equipment, and built a reputation across North Texas for being the company people call when they want the job done right the first time.

By the Numbers

Years in Business

What We Stand For

The Direct Pumping
Standard

🤝

Honest & Straightforward

We tell you what we find, what it means, and what it costs. No upselling, no fear tactics, no inflated estimates. Just the facts.

🕐

We Show Up On Time

Your time matters. We give you a real arrival window and we stick to it. If something changes, we call you — not the other way around.

🏆

Quality Over Shortcuts

We do the job right the first time. Cutting corners costs you more in the long run, and that's not how we want to do business.

📍

Local & Accountable

We live and work in North Texas. Our reputation is built here, and we're accountable to this community in a way a national chain never could be.

🌿

Environmentally Responsible

All waste is disposed of at licensed facilities in full compliance with TCEQ regulations. We take our environmental responsibility seriously.

📞

Real Communication

You can actually reach us. We answer calls, respond to messages, and treat every customer like the neighbors they are.

Service Area

North Texas &
Growing

We're based in Johnson County and currently serve the greater North Texas region. As we grow, we're expanding our footprint into Central Texas to bring the same level of service to more communities.

  • 📍
    Headquarters: Johnson County, TX
  • 🗺️

  • 🚀

Ready to Work With Us?

Whether you need a one-time septic pump, a recurring service contract, or an inspection for a real estate transaction — we're ready to help. Reach out and we'll get back to you fast.

Licensed & Fully Insured

Direct Pumping is fully licensed and insured for all services we provide in the state of Texas. Ask us for documentation — we're happy to provide it.

    Wastewater Treatment
    Plant Services

    Sludge removal, lift station service, emergency response, and scheduled maintenance for municipal, industrial, and utility-owned treatment facilities across North Texas.

    📞 Call Now
    What We Do

    Heavy-Duty Service
    For Treatment Facilities

    Wastewater treatment plants are complex, high-demand operations — and when something goes wrong, it can't wait. Direct Pumping provides the specialized pumping services that keep treatment facilities running clean, compliant, and on schedule. We handle the dirty work so your plant keeps moving.

    • 🏗️
      Sludge Removal & Hauling — Thorough removal of accumulated sludge from digesters, clarifiers, holding basins, and lagoons. All material transported and disposed of at licensed facilities in full TCEQ compliance.
    • ⚙️
      Lift Station Service On-Site — Wet well pumping, cleaning, and maintenance for all lift stations within your treatment facility. We keep your internal pump infrastructure operating at full capacity.
    • 🚨
      Emergency Response & Bypass Pumping — Equipment failure, storm events, or unexpected overflow? We respond fast with bypass pumping capability to keep your plant operational while repairs are made.
    • 📅
      Scheduled Maintenance Contracts — Recurring service agreements tailored to your facility's volume, schedule, and regulatory requirements. We show up when we're supposed to — every time.

    Why Plants Choose Direct Pumping

    Treatment plant managers tell us the same things matter most:

    ▸ Reliable scheduling — we show up when committed
    ▸ Proper waste manifests for every job
    ▸ 24/7 emergency availability — not just business hours
    ▸ Equipment capable of handling high-volume jobs
    ▸ Crews who understand plant operations and protocols

    Service Details

    Every Service,
    Done to Standard

    🏗️

    Sludge Removal & Hauling

    We pump accumulated biosolids, sludge, and residuals from digesters, holding basins, clarifiers, and lagoons. All material is manifested and transported to licensed TCEQ-permitted disposal facilities. Documentation provided for every load.

    ⚙️

    On-Site Lift Station Service

    Most treatment facilities operate multiple internal lift stations. We provide full wet well pumping, cleaning, and inspection for all on-site stations as part of a comprehensive maintenance plan or standalone service visit.

    🚨

    Emergency & Bypass Pumping

    When a pump fails, a line backs up, or storm inflow overwhelms your capacity, we mobilize fast. Bypass pumping keeps your process running while the primary system is repaired — minimizing overflow risk and regulatory exposure.

    📋

    Compliance Documentation

    Every service visit produces proper manifests, disposal records, and service documentation — exactly what you need for TCEQ reporting, permit audits, and internal compliance records.

    📅

    Maintenance Contracts

    Custom recurring service agreements built around your facility's load, schedule, and regulatory calendar. We track your service history and show up when we're supposed to — no chasing required.

    📞

    24/7 On-Call Availability

    Treatment plants don't operate on a 9-to-5 schedule, and neither do we. Our emergency line is staffed around the clock for situations that can't wait until morning.

    Compliance & Documentation

    We Keep Your
    Records Clean

    Wastewater treatment facilities operate under strict TCEQ permits and reporting requirements. Every job Direct Pumping performs at your facility comes with the documentation you need to stay compliant — no chasing us for paperwork after the fact.

    • 📋
      Waste transport manifests for every load hauled
    • 🏭
      Disposal at TCEQ-permitted facilities only
    • 📁
      Service records available for permit audits
    • ✅
      Crews trained on plant safety and access protocols
    • 🔒
      Fully licensed and insured for all work performed

    Ready to Set Up a Service Contract?

    Most of our treatment plant customers are on recurring contracts. It's the simplest way to stay compliant, budget predictably, and never have to think about scheduling. Call us or request a quote and we'll put together a proposal tailored to your facility.

    Let's Talk About Your Facility

    Serving municipal, industrial, utility, and construction wastewater facilities across North Texas.
